name: Draconic Ember
description: You snap your fingers, tracing a spark-sigil. A mote of draconic flame bolts toward a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 fire damage. The flame ignites flammable objects not being worn or carried. The sigil drawn in the air resembles your bloodline's ancestral crest, burning briefly before the ember launches.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d10 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Caustic Spittle
description: You trace the sigil of the Venom-Dragon across your lips and spit a globule of caustic aether-bile. Choose one creature within range or two creatures within 5 feet of each other. Each target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 acid damage. The bile hisses and smokes where it lands, etching faint draconic runes into stone before fading.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d6 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Iron-Scale Ward
description: You trace the sigil of the Iron Dragon across your own flesh, and the ghostly shimmer of draconic scales ripples over your skin. Until the end of your next turn, you have resistance against bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage dealt by weapon attacks. The sigil glows faintly beneath your skin, visible as faintly luminous scale patterns when struck.

name: Grave-Cold Grasp
description: You utter a syllable of the Old Tongue reserved for the dead, and a spectral draconic claw of chill aether reaches from the underworld to clutch at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 necrotic damage and cannot regain hit points until the start of your next turn. If the target is undead, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the end of your next turn. The claw is bleached white, as though drained of all warmth by ages spent below.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Wyrm-Wisp
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Fire or Wild)
description: You trace four small wing-sigils in the air, and four faint draconic spirits manifest as floating flame-orbs. They resemble tiny hovering wyrms, each shedding dim light in a 10-foot radius. As a bonus action, you can command the wisps to drift up to 60 feet. Each wisp winks out if it passes beyond 120 feet from you. The wisps emit a soft, rhythmic hum that echo-channelers believe is the lullaby of a long-dead Star Dragon.

name: Wild-Blood Sign
description: You whisper the Old Tongue names of the Primordial Dragons — those ancient wyrms who became the land itself — and nature responds with a minor sign. You create one of the following effects: a harmless sensory effect (the scent of petrichor, a distant draconic rumble), an instant lighting or snuffing of a small flame, a brief whisper of weather prediction for the next 24 hours, or the blooming or wilting of a small plant. The effect feels ancient and instinctual, as though the world's blood remembers its creators.

name: Aether Lance
description: You trace the sigil of the Void-Dragon and a beam of crackling aether energy erupts from your finger. The raw, untethered power of your bloodline — stripped of element, pure force of will — screams across the battlefield. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 force damage. The beam's color shifts between every hue of dragon breath known to the Old Tongue.
higher_level: The spell creates more beams at higher levels: two beams at 5th level, three at 11th, and four at 17th. You can direct the beams at the same target or different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.

name: Bloodline Whisper
description: You touch a willing creature and speak a single word of ancestral encouragement in the Old Tongue. A faint echo of your draconic forebears offers a moment of precognitive insight. Once within the duration, the target can add 1d4 to one ability check of its choice. The whisper feels like a warm talon resting gently on the back of the mind — many casters describe it as hearing their grandmother's voice.

name: Sovereign's Glow
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Fire or Radiant)
description: You touch an object and trace the sigil of the Sun Dragon upon it. The object sheds bright aether-light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. The light is the color of the caster's bloodline flame — crimson, azure, pale gold, or void-black. Any creature within the bright light who conceals their bloodline feels an uncomfortable warmth, as if being watched. The spell ends if cast again or dismissed as an action.

name: Spectral Claw
description: You trace the sigil of a grasping talon, and a floating, semi-transparent draconic claw manifests at a point within range. The claw lasts for the duration and can be moved up to 30 feet as an action. It can manipulate objects, open unlocked doors, pour vials, or retrieve items — but cannot attack, activate magical items, or carry more than 10 pounds. The claw's shape echoes your bloodline: crimson and hooked for fire lineages, pale and slender for rime, shadow-wreathed for void.

name: Scale-Knit
description: You trace the sigil of renewal — two linked crescents forming a scale pattern — upon a broken object. A single break or tear up to 1 foot in any dimension seals with a faint aetheric shimmer, as if the object's own essence remembers wholeness. The repair leaves no scar, though discerning eyes can spot a faint optical distortion along the mend. In draconic legend, this sigil was gifted to mortals by the Gem-Dragon who first taught craft to the world.

name: Old-Tongue Whisper
description: You point a finger at a creature within range while whispering Old Tongue syllables. Your words travel on aether currents, inaudible to all but the intended recipient. The target hears the message as a whisper inside their mind, spoken in the voice of a dragon — resonant, ancient, and impossible to ignore. The target can reply with a whisper you alone hear. The message cannot pass through solid stone thicker than 1 foot, and the spell is blocked by active Inquisitor wards.

name: Aether-Mirage
description: You trace a shimmering sigil in the air, and a wisp of aether coalesces into a simple illusion — either a sound or an image of an object no larger than a 5-foot cube. The illusion is as fragile as heat-haze over a dragon's lair: physical interaction reveals it immediately, and any creature that examines it closely recognizes the faint, ever-shifting iridescence of unshaped aether. The Old Tongue contains ninety-seven words for 'illusion' — each reflecting a different draconic philosophy on the nature of reality.

name: Venom-Breath Sigil
description: You trace the crest of the Venom-Dragon across your throat and exhale. A puff of toxic aether-gas — thick as a wyrm's dying breath — clouds toward a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d12 poison damage. The cloud's color betrays your bloodline: emerald for forest-dragons, violet for deep-dragons, bone-white for carrion lineages.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d12 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Lesser Sigil-Craft
description: You trace a rapid series of minor sigils — the first tricks any bloodline-child learns. Choose one of the following effects: create a harmless sensory effect (sparks from your fingertips, a brief draconic chord); light or snuff a candle-sized flame; clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot; warm, chill, or flavor nonliving material; make a small mark, symbol, or bloodline crest appear on a surface for 1 hour; or conjure a small trinket that fits in your palm and lasts until the end of your next turn. Casters with higher purity find their Lesser Sigil-Craft effects more vivid — a 50%+ purity caster's illusory crests pulse with actual heat.

name: Fang-Fire
description: You cup your palm and trace a spark-sigil within it. A flickering draconic flame kindles in your hand — warm as a hatchling's first breath. The flame sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. As an action, you can hurl the flame at a creature within 30 feet; make a ranged spell attack, dealing 1d8 fire damage on a hit. The flame extinguishes when thrown, but rekindles if you trace the sigil again. Wild-Bloods use this flame to light campfires by speaking the Fire-Dragon's name into the kindling.
higher_level: The spell's thrown damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Frost-Wyrm's Gaze
description: You fix your gaze on a creature and trace the sigil of the Rime-Dragon. A beam of freezing aether — cold as the stare of the White Wyrm who sleeps beneath the northern ice — streaks toward the target. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 cold damage and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. The ground beneath the beam's path momentarily frosts over with delicate, draconic-scale patterns.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Scale-Steel Resolve
description: You trace the sigil of the Adamant Dragon upon a willing creature. Its bloodline briefly awakens, hardening the creature's connection to the material plane. Once within the duration, the target can add 1d4 to one saving throw of its choice. The target's skin momentarily takes on a faint metallic sheen when it draws on this reserve — the chromatic flash of a dragon's underbelly glimpsed through water.

name: Dragon-God's Gaze
description: You utter one of the ninety-nine Names of the Dragon-Gods, and a column of searing radiance descends as though one of them has turned its eye upon a sinner. A creature you can see within range must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d8 radiant damage. The target gains no benefit from cover for this saving throw. The light is not fire — it is the absolute attention of divinity, and it burns only what it judges. Inquisitors recognize this spell by the eye-shaped afterimage it leaves on the ground.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Fang-Staff
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Earth or Wild)
description: You trace the sigil of the Primal Fang upon a wooden club or quarterstaff. The wood hardens with draconic essence — the weapon becomes magical, and you can use your spellcasting ability modifier instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls. On a hit, the weapon deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage (becoming a d8 if smaller). The wood darkens, its grain shifting into a pattern reminiscent of dragonbone. Druids of the Wild-Blood consider this the first spell taught to every acolyte.

name: Storm-Wyrm's Touch
description: Your hand crackles with the lightning of a Storm-Dragon's breath as you trace a jagged sigil in the air and lunge. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 lightning damage and cannot take reactions until the start of its next turn. You have advantage on the attack roll if the target is wearing metal armor. The crackling discharge arcs across the target's armor like tiny serpents — the Storm-Wyrm's children, according to sailor folklore.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Timeless Breath
description: You exhale a whisper of the Eternal Dragon's breath — the one legend says sleeps at the center of all time — onto a living creature that has 0 hit points. The creature becomes stable. The spell has no effect on constructs or undead. Those revived by Timeless Breath often report a fleeting vision: a vast, coiled dragon of impossible size, one eye open and glowing with patient, infinite calm.

name: Dragon-Awe
description: Your voice resonates with the latent authority of your bloodline, carrying the weight of ancient wyrms. You create one of the following effects within range: your voice booms three times as loud for up to 1 minute; flames within the area flicker and change color to match your bloodline hue; the ground tremors faintly like the footfall of something vast; an unopened door or window flies open or slams shut; your eyes blaze with draconic iridescence for 1 minute; or you project a brief roar — the kind that, in the Old Tongue, precedes the word for 'kneel.'

name: Predator's Mark
description: You fix your draconic gaze on a creature within range and trace the sigil of the Hunt. Your bloodline — ancient predator instinct distilled — guides your next strike. On your next turn, you gain advantage on your first attack roll against the target, provided the spell hasn't ended. The target feels a prickling at the back of its neck, as though a wyrm circles unseen overhead, choosing the moment to dive.

name: Aether-Blade Sigil
description: You trace a blade-sigil along your weapon's edge, and the weapon wreathes in aether-fire — the color of your bloodline's flame. As part of casting, make a melee weapon attack against a creature within 5 feet. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects, and draconic fire leaps from the target to a second creature you can see within 5 feet. The second creature takes fire damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier. Martial Archons and Dragon-Heirs favor this spell — it was first taught to mortal warriors by a Crimson Dragon who admired their courage.
higher_level: The second creature's fire damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level, plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

name: Thunderclaw Strike
description: You trace a resonant sigil along your weapon, and it hums with contained thunder-aether — the roar of a Storm-Dragon trapped in steel. As part of casting, make a melee weapon attack against a creature within 5 feet. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and is sheathed in crackling thunder-aether. If the target willingly moves 5 feet or more before your next turn, the sigil detonates: the target takes 1d8 thunder damage. The sigil's hum is audible to anyone within 10 feet — a warning the wise learn to recognize.
higher_level: The detonation damage increases by 1d8 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Crimson Lash
description: You draw a line across your palm with a blade, flick the welling blood into the air, and trace a barb-sigil through it. The blood crystallizes into a barbed lash of crimson aether that whips through a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the cone must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 slashing damage. You take 1 necrotic damage when you cast this spell; this damage cannot be reduced and does not trigger concentration saves from the spell itself. Crimson Oath hunters call this their 'first lesson' — the day they learn their own blood is the sharpest weapon.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d6 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Blood-Tithe Dart
description: You prick your thumb on the edge of your Aether-Core, and a single droplet of blood crystallizes into a needle-sharp dart of seeking crimson aether. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature within range. On a hit, the target takes 1d4 piercing damage plus 1d4 necrotic damage. You take 1 necrotic damage when you cast this spell; this damage cannot be reduced. The dart veers slightly mid-flight — those with draconic blood instinctively flinch as it passes, recognizing the signature of a hunter.
higher_level: The piercing and necrotic damage each increase by 1d4 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Spark-Wire
description: You flick a thin copper wire from a wrist-mounted gadget and the wire unspools through the air, guided by a lightning-sigil you trace with your other hand. The wire delivers a jolt of stored aether-electricity to a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 lightning damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or its speed is halved until the start of your next turn. Aether-Smiths who favor this spell call it their 'handshake' — the first prototype every apprentice builds.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d6 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

name: Aether-Core Pulse
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Thunder or Wild)
description: You overcharge your equipped Aether-Core by slamming your palm against it and shouting a resonant syllable of the Old Tongue. A pulse of concussive aether radiates from you in a 10-foot cube. Each creature in the area other than you must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet directly away from you and take 1d4 thunder damage. The core vents a brief plume of shimmering exhaust — the same color as the dragon whose essence it holds.
higher_level: The spell's damage increases by 1d4 at 5th, 11th, and 17th level, and the push distance increases by 5 feet at 11th level.

name: Blood-Sense
description: You close your eyes and focus inward, listening to the rhythm of your own draconic blood. That rhythm extends outward, resonating with the aether in other living creatures. For the duration, you sense the presence and location of any creature with draconic blood (purity 1% or higher) within 60 feet. You also discern their purity bracket (Tainted, Lesser Blood, Half-Sovereign, etc.) but not their identity. Creatures with 0% purity are invisible to this sense. This spell was developed by the Dragon-Heirs of the Obsidian Court to sniff out hidden bloodlines — and hidden hunters.

name: Sovereign's Hiss
description: You speak a single, sharp syllable of the Old Tongue — the sound a dragon makes when its patience has run dry. A creature of your choice within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be wracked with instinctual dread: it has disadvantage on its next attack roll against you before the end of its next turn. The syllable lingers in the air like frost-breath on winter glass. Even those who understand no Old Tongue recognize the meaning: 'remember your place.'

name: Bloodline Benediction
description: You trace the sigil of the Celestial Dragon and utter a blessing in the Old Tongue, awakening the latent draconic essence in up to three creatures of your choice within range. Each target gains a +1d4 bonus to attack rolls and saving throws for the duration. This is not prayer — it is blood speaking to blood, older than any god. The sigil traced in the air leaves a faint golden afterimage visible only to those currently under its effect.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

name: Wyrm's Exhalation
description: You trace the fire-dragon's breath-sigil across your throat and roar. A sheet of draconic flame erupts from your outstretched hands in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw; a creature takes 3d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The flames are the exact hue of your bloodline — a knowledgeable observer can identify your lineage by the color alone. Flammable objects in the area ignite unless worn or carried.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Sovereign's Word
description: You speak a humanoid's name in the Old Tongue, lacing it with the subtle authority of your bloodline. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw; if it fails, it regards you as a friendly acquaintance for the duration. The effect ends if you or your companions do anything harmful to it. When the spell ends, the creature knows it was influenced by draconic compulsion — it felt the weight of a wyrm's presence behind your words. Creatures with 50%+ purity have advantage on the save; they recognize the Old Tongue for what it is.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

name: Old-Tongue Edict
description: You speak a single, irreducible word of command in the Old Tongue — the language dragons used to shape the first world. A creature of your choice within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose from: Approach, Drop, Flee, Grovel, Halt, or Kneel. The command is not heard as sound but as a pressure in the target's blood — an ancestral instinct to obey that predates mammalian evolution.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can affect one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when targeted.

name: Bloodline Lexicon
description: You draw a circle-sigil on your forehead and whisper the Old Tongue word for 'understand.' Your bloodline awakens its genetic memory of the first language — the tongue all mortal speech descended from. For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any spoken language you hear and any written language you see, provided you are touching the text. You must be in physical contact with the surface of the writing or the speaker. This spell is a ritual of the Lore-Keepers, who use it to translate lost bloodline records.

name: Sanguine Mending
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Radiant or Wild)
description: You place your palm on a creature and trace the sigil of the Life-Dragon — the legendary wyrm said to have shed blood to create the first mortal race. Warm aether flows through your veins into theirs. The target regains hit points equal to 1d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier. The touch leaves a faint, temporary scale-pattern glow on the skin where the wound was — a mark that fades within the hour. Undead and constructs are unaffected; the Life-Dragon's gift is for the living.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Aether-Sense
description: You trace the sigil of the All-Seeing Dragon — an ancient wyrm of legend whose thousand eyes watched the forging of every plane — over your own eyes. For the duration, you sense the presence of magic within 30 feet. If you detect magic, you can use an action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object that bears magic, and you identify which draconic element the magic is associated with (fire, lightning, void, radiant, etc.). Active Inquisitor wards appear as a sickly gray static in your aether-vision.

name: Aether-Glamour
description: You trace a shroud-sigil around your body, and a shimmer of aether wraps you in an illusory guise. You appear as any humanoid of your size, including clothing and equipment, up to 1 foot taller or shorter. The illusion doesn't hold up to physical inspection — a creature touching you feels the real you beneath. To those with the Aether-Sense spell active, the glamour appears as a translucent second skin, like a dragonfly's wing draped over you. Fugitive casters in Inquisitor-patrolled cities favor this spell above all others.

name: Primal-Scale Snare
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Earth or Wild)
description: You trace the sigil of the Root-Dragon — one of the Primordials who became the first forests — upon the ground. Grasping vines and roots, their surfaces ridged with scale-like bark patterns, erupt from the earth in a 20-foot square centered on a point within range. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained. A restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC, freeing itself on a success. The vines pulse slowly, as though breathing — a heartbeat left behind by the world's oldest dragon.

name: Dragon-Glow Brand
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Fire or Radiant)
description: You trace a flare-sigil and hurl a sphere of iridescent draconic light to a point within range. Each creature in a 20-foot cube within that point must make a Dexterity saving throw or be outlined in shimmering light — the color of your bloodline's flame — for the duration. Affected creatures shed dim light in a 10-foot radius and cannot benefit from invisibility. Attack rolls against them have advantage. The light outlines the creature's skeleton in faintly glowing lines, as though their bones momentarily recall a draconic shape.

name: Wyrm-Wing Cushion
casting_time: 1 reaction (taken when you or a creature within 60 feet of you falls)
description: You trace the sigil of the Wind-Dragon in a swift, panicked arc. A cushion of shimmering aether — visible as a brief, translucent pair of spectral dragon wings — envelops up to five falling creatures within range. Their rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round for the duration. If they land before the spell ends, they take no falling damage and land on their feet. The wings dissipate on landing, leaving behind the faint scent of ozone and a lingering warmth.

name: Lesser Wyrm-Ling
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Wild, plus herbs and incense worth 10 GC)
description: You trace the sigil of a hatchling and speak the Old Tongue name of a tiny draconic spirit. The spirit assumes the form of a Tiny celestial, fey, or fiend — whatever shape your bloodline's patron-dragon favors — and manifests within range. It acts independently but always obeys your commands. In combat, it shares your initiative and takes its own turn. The spirit is intelligent, speaks one language of your choice in the rasping tones of a baby dragon, and can deliver touch spells on your behalf. When reduced to 0 hit points, it disperses into a puff of iridescent aether smoke. You may resummon it by casting this spell again. Archon Casters record these spirits' true names in their grimoires.

name: Breath-Shroud
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Water or Air)
description: You trace the sigil of the Fog-Dragon and exhale slowly. A thick, obscuring mist — like the breath of a sleeping wyrm — billows from a point within range, forming a 20-foot-radius sphere. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for the duration or until dispersed by a strong wind. The mist carries a faint, ancient scent: brine and thunder and something reptilian, as though the air remembers what it was before dragons breathed life into the world.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the radius of the sphere increases by 20 feet for each slot level above 1st.

name: Sovereign's Bolt
description: You trace a lance-sigil and hurl a bolt of concentrated draconic radiance at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 radiant damage and is wreathed in a corona of lingering aether-light until the end of your next turn. The next attack roll made against the target before the corona fades has advantage. The bolt travels in a straight line, burning a brief, eye-shaped afterimage into the air — the mark of the Dragon-Gods' attention.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Life-Sigil Hymn
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Radiant or Wild)
description: You trace a brief, three-note sigil in the air — each note a syllable of the Life-Dragon's name — and a pulse of warm aether arcs to a creature within range. The target regains hit points equal to 1d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier. The spell can be cast as a bonus action, making it invaluable in the chaos of battle. The target hears a faint, harmonic chord when the aether strikes — the same chord that Lore-Keepers say echoed when the first dragon drew breath.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 1d4 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Wyrm-Fire Heart
description: You trace the heart-sigil — an ancient symbol shaped like a dragon's cardiac chamber — on a willing creature's chest. Their heart burns with borrowed draconic courage. The target gains temporary hit points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each of its turns for the duration, and it is immune to the frightened condition. When the spell ends, the target loses any remaining temporary hit points. The target's heartbeat is audible to anyone within 5 feet — a deep, rhythmic drum like the pacing of a caged wyrm.

name: Aether-Reading
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Wild, plus a pearl worth 100 GC that the spell consumes)
description: You touch an object and trace the sigil of Knowing over it. The pearl — crushed to powder and sprinkled on the object — dissolves into aether-threads that weave through its history. You learn the object's magical properties, how to use them, whether it requires attunement, how many charges it has (if any), and whether any spells are affecting it. You also learn which draconic element the item's creator's bloodline was attuned to. This is not divination in the conventional sense — the object's own aether-memory speaks to you.

name: Blood-Rend
description: You trace the rend-sigil — a jagged claw-mark that smolders black — and rake your hand through the target's life-force. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 3d10 necrotic damage, and you feel a brief, intoxicating surge of stolen vitality through your veins. The wound left behind is bloodless but black-veined, as though the target's own draconic blood (however thin) recoils from the violation. This spell is forbidden by the Citadel and taught only in the hidden Orders of the Crimson Oath.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Dragon-Leg Surge
description: You trace the leaping-sigil — two crescents like a dragon's hind-legs coiled to spring — on a willing creature's calves. The target's muscles surge with aether-imbued power. For the duration, the target's jump distance is tripled. The target's legs momentarily shimmer with a faint scale pattern when they jump, and their landing produces a sound deeper than their weight should allow — a muffled thud that echoes the landings of things much larger.

name: Scale-Stride
description: You trace the stride-sigil — a flowing, elongated mark like a dragon's gait stretched across a landscape — on a willing creature. The target's speed increases by 10 feet for the duration. The aether that suffuses their legs leaves faintly luminous footprints for moments after each step, visible only in darkness. Striders of the wild orders use this spell to cover vast distances, leaving behind trails that rival the patrol routes of young dragons.

name: Aether-Scale Shroud
description: You trace the armor-sigil across your body, and a translucent layer of shimmering draconic scales — invisible to the naked eye but solid to weapons — wraps around you. Until the spell ends, the target's base AC becomes 13 + its Dexterity modifier if not wearing armor. The spell ends if the target dons armor or is dismissed as an action. When struck, faint scale-pattern ripples flash across the target's skin, visible in that split second as the color of the caster's bloodline.

name: Sigil Dart
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Force or Wild)
description: You trace three small dart-sigils in rapid succession, and three crystalline darts of pure aether-force shoot from your fingertip. Each dart unerringly strikes a creature of your choice within range, dealing 1d4 + 1 force damage. The darts can be directed at one target or spread among several. They leave faint, smoking trails in the air that smell of ozone and rust, and their impacts sound like ice cracking — the sound, according to legend, of the Void-Dragon hatching from the shell of the old universe.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you create one additional dart for each slot level above 1st.

name: Sanctified Blood-Ward
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Radiant, plus a pinch of iron filings consumed by the spell)
description: You trace a warding circle-sigil on a willing creature and scatter iron filings that hang suspended in the air, forming a protective ring. Until the spell ends, aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against the target. The target also cannot be charmed, frightened, or possessed by them. The warding circle glows with the steady, patient light of a dragon guarding its hoard. The iron filings were chosen for their symbolic resonance — dragons loathe the taste of cold iron, and so do the things that hunt in the spaces between planes.

name: Cleansing Flame Sigil
description: You trace the flame of purification — a dragon's tongue-sigil — over food and drink within a 5-foot-radius sphere. All nonmagical food and drink within the area is purified and rendered free of poison and disease. A faint wisp of chromatic smoke rises from the purified items, and for a moment, the food tastes as though it has been warmed by a dragon's breath — rich, savory, faintly spiced. Ordained of the Citadel perform this rite before every communal meal.

name: Wyrm-Rest
description: You trace a stillness-sigil over a creature within range. The aether around them thickens into a protective shimmer. Until the spell ends, any creature who targets the warded creature with an attack or harmful spell must first make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the attacker must choose a new target or lose the attack or spell. The spell ends if the warded creature makes an attack, casts a spell that affects an enemy, or deals damage to another creature. The shimmer takes the form of a coiled dragon, tail-to-snout, encircling the warded creature protectively.

name: Scale-Reflex
casting_time: 1 reaction (taken when you are hit by an attack or targeted by the magic missile spell)
description: You trace a defensive sigil in a blur of instinct. An invisible barrier of hardened aether — like a patch of dragon-scale made manifest — flashes into existence and intercepts the incoming attack. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +5 bonus to AC, including against the triggering attack, and you take no damage from the Sigil Dart spell. The barrier appears as a brief, translucent scale-shield of your bloodline's color, visible only in the instant of impact.

name: Aegis of the Bloodline
components: V, S, M (Fractured Aether-Core — Earth or Radiant)
description: You trace the aegis-sigil — a dragon's profile in silhouette, facing outward — and hurl it at a creature within range. A shimmering draconic field encases the target. The target gains a +2 bonus to AC for the duration. When the aegis absorbs a blow, a brief, musical tone rings out — the note varies by bloodline, and some Ordained claim to identify a stranger's lineage by the pitch of their aegis.

name: Phantom-Wyrm
description: You trace an image-sigil and a phantom wyrm — no larger than a horse, no smaller than a cat — materializes in a 15-foot cube within range. It is purely visual, has no substance, and makes no sound. The image looks and moves convincingly: a draconic form of your design, scaled and winged, breathing silent, illusory flame. Physical interaction reveals it as illusion. You can move the image up to 30 feet as an action. Inquisitors train to spot Phantom-Wyrms; they know real dragons do not blink at the same rhythm as illusions.

name: Wyrm-Lull
description: You trace a gentle, looping sigil — the same one dragon-mothers use to calm their hatchlings — and hum a low, resonant chord in the Old Tongue. Creatures within a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range fall into a magical slumber, provided they have 5d8 hit points or fewer (rolled when the spell is cast). Creatures are affected in ascending order of current hit points, and the spell ends for a creature if it takes damage or someone uses an action to shake it awake. Creatures with 25%+ purity have advantage on the saving throw; their blood recognizes the lullaby and fights it. Undead and creatures immune to charm are unaffected.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the hit point pool increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Feral-Kin Tongue
description: You trace the beast-sigil — a stylized dragon's claw print — on your throat. For the duration, you can comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts. The knowledge and awareness of beasts is limited by their intelligence, but they will speak to you willingly, recognizing in your voice the echo of the Primordial Dragons who created all living things. Most beasts speak in simple images and emotions; a wolf might show you 'fur-that-walks-upright-with-metal-stick' rather than 'a guard with a spear.'

name: Drake's Mockery
description: You trace a mock-sigil — a crude caricature of a dragon's face with its tongue out — and speak a string of absurdities in the Old Tongue that somehow translate into the target's deepest insecurities. A creature you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall into a fit of uncontrollable, incapacitating laughter and fall prone. At the end of each of its turns and each time it takes damage, the target can make another Wisdom saving throw. The spell ends on a success. Creatures with Intelligence 4 or less are immune. Dragons consider this spell profoundly offensive — it was first created by a Lore-Keeper who survived a red wyrm's monologue.

name: Thunder-Wyrm's Roar
description: You trace the roar-sigil — a jagged, expanding spiral — and roar with the voice of the Thunder-Wyrm. A wave of thunderous aether erupts from you in a 15-foot cube. Each creature other than you in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 2d8 thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet directly away from you. On a success, it takes half damage and isn't pushed. Unsecured objects in the area are automatically pushed 10 feet. The roar echoes through the aether for miles; any dragon within 50 miles hears it — and may answer.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Phantom Attendant
description: You trace the servant-sigil — a stylized talon holding a cup — and a shimmering, invisible draconic spirit manifests at a point within range. The attendant is shapeless, invisible, and mindless; it performs simple tasks at your command: fetching objects, opening doors, cleaning, folding, mending, pouring, stoking fires. It cannot attack, and it dissipates if it takes any damage or moves more than 60 feet from you. Lore-Keepers use Phantom Attendants to organize vast archives of bloodline records; the spirits never complain about the dust.

name: Sanguine Geas
description: You slice your palm open with a ritual blade (taking 1d6 slashing damage that cannot be reduced) and trace a binding-sigil in your own blood. A creature you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is under a lesser blood-geas for the duration: you may speak a single command as a bonus action on each of your turns, which the target must follow to the best of its ability unless the command is directly self-destructive. Each time the target takes damage, it may repeat the saving throw. The geas appears as faint crimson strands connecting your bleeding palm to the target's temples — visible only to those with active Blood-Sense or Aether-Sense.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the duration increases by 1 minute for each slot level above 1st.

name: Core Overload
casting_time: 1 reaction (taken when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack)
description: You slam your palm against your Aether-Core, overloading its safety governor. The core vents violently, discharging a crackling burst of aether-lightning. The attacking creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes 2d8 lightning damage and is dazed — its next attack roll before the end of its next turn has disadvantage. On a success, it takes half damage and is not dazed. The core's venting produces a sharp crack and a puff of acrid smoke that smells of copper and burnt scale. Aether-Smiths who overuse this spell find their cores degrade faster than expected.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.

name: Bloodline Surge
description: You slam your fist against your chest and roar a single syllable of the Old Tongue — the word dragons use to demand their blood obey. Your purity surges. For the duration, your effective purity increases by 10%, reducing your Spell Safety DC accordingly, and you gain a +1d4 bonus to spell attack rolls. However, your veins burn with unstable power: when the spell ends, you suffer 2d6 psychic damage as Aether Burn from the backlash. This damage cannot be reduced. This spell cannot raise your purity above 100%. Dragon-Heirs consider Bloodline Surge the mark of a true Sovereign — the willingness to burn for power.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the purity bonus increases by +5% and the spell attack bonus increases by +1 for each slot level above 1st, but the backlash damage increases by 1d6 for each additional slot level.

name: Sworn Retribution
casting_time: 1 reaction (taken when a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see damages an ally of yours)
description: You speak the Oath-Word — a single syllable in the Old Tongue that binds all Oath-Sworn to their vows — and your bloodline answers with righteous fury. The triggering creature is marked by a burning sigil that hovers above its head, visible to all. Until the end of your next turn, your weapon attacks against the marked creature deal an additional 1d8 radiant damage. The mark's shape reflects your Oath: a scale for Devotion, a fang for Vengeance, a claw for Conquest. The creature feels the weight of the Oath pressing on its soul — a suffocating heat that intensifies the closer you get.

name: Hunter's Aether-Mark
description: You trace the tracking-sigil — a spiraling claw-mark that narrows to a point — and hurl it at a creature you can see. The sigil sinks into the target's aether-signature like a thorn. For the duration, you always know the direction and approximate distance to the marked creature, provided it is on the same plane of existence. The mark cannot be removed except by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic, but it fades naturally when the duration expires. A marked creature with 25%+ purity may feel a faint itch between the shoulder blades — the ancestral memory of being hunted by dragons.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the duration extends to 24 hours (3rd-4th), 7 days (5th-6th), or until dispelled (7th+).
